title: Citrusy Silky Algerian Tofu Fritters
description: Crispy, citrus-infused tofu fritters with a silky tahini drizzle, perfect for a cozy, kosher-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: extra-firm tofu
ingredients.notes: press for 15 minutes to remove excess moisture

ingredients.name: finely chopped spring onions (green parts only)
tahini
ingredients.notes: add 1 tbsp water and 1 tsp lemon juice to make sauce

instructions: Press the tofu for 15 minutes to remove excess moisture, then crumble into a large mixing bowl.
Add the all-purpose flour, chickpea flour, cumin, paprika, salt, pepper, finely chopped spring onions, lemon zest, and fresh lemon juice; mix until a thick, cohesive batter forms.
Cover the batter and let it rest at room temperature for 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and the batter to thicken.
Heat 1 tbsp of the olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat; once hot, drop spoonfuls of the batter into the skillet, flattening each with the back of a spoon.
Cook the fritters for 3–4 minutes per side, or until golden brown and crisp; transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
While the fritters finish cooking, whisk the tahini with 1 tbsp water, 1 tsp lemon juice, and a pinch of salt until smooth and silky; adjust consistency with a little more water if needed.
Plate the crispy fritters and drizzle the silky tahini sauce over them; garnish with additional fresh spring onion green parts if desired.
Serve warm as a satisfying lunch or snack.
